deliverable: Biostatistics input to protocol — design rationale & study design selection
description: Biostatistician's written contribution to the protocol justifying the overall design (e.g., randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, sequential-cohort SAD/MAD; sentinel dosing; cohort size; within-cohort active:placebo ratio such as 6:2 or 8:2; intra-/inter-subject design; crossover vs parallel for food-effect/DDI/relative-BA; fixed-sequence vs Williams-square crossover). Articulates why the design answers the objectives and controls bias (randomization, blinding, washout adequacy vs t1/2).
inputs: Target Product Profile / development plan; nonclinical PK/tox (NOAEL, exposure margins, predicted human PK, t1/2); prior compound/class data; clinical pharmacology objectives; regulatory guidance (FDA FIH max-starting-dose, EMA FIH/early-phase guideline)
outputs: Design section of the protocol; feeds randomization spec, SAP design assumptions, dose-escalation rules, sample-size rationale
standard: ICH M11 (CeSHarP protocol template) design section; ICH E9 (design principles, bias control); ICH E6(R3) GCP; FDA/EMA FIH guidance
qc: Independent biostatistician peer review; cross-check design vs objectives/estimands; medical/clin-pharm/regulatory review; protocol team consensus
ai_use: Cloud Claude (de-identified, no PHI) drafts/redlines design-rationale prose, summarizes class precedents and regulatory guidance, and stress-tests internal consistency of design vs objectives; all design decisions and final wording are human-owned by the biostatistician. No subject-level data involved.

deliverable: Objectives → estimands → endpoints alignment (ICH E9(R1) estimand framework)
description: Structured mapping of each study objective to a target estimand and to the corresponding endpoint(s). For each primary/key-secondary objective, defines the five estimand attributes: treatment condition, population, variable/endpoint, intercurrent-event (ICE) handling strategy, and population-level summary. In Phase 1 clin-pharm, most objectives are descriptive (PK NCA parameters, safety/tolerability), so estimands are largely descriptive 'treatment-policy'-style with explicit ICE strategies for dropouts, dose interruptions, emesis (PK exclusion), concomitant-med use, and AE-driven discontinuation; comparative estimands (GMR + 90% CI) apply for food-effect/DDI/BE substudies.
inputs: Protocol objectives; endpoint definitions; PK/PD variable list; anticipated intercurrent events (emesis, dosing errors, missed doses, rescue/concomitant meds, discontinuation); clinical input on clinical questions of interest
outputs: Estimand subsection of the protocol statistical-considerations section; drives analysis-set definitions, ICE-handling rules in SAP, and TLF planning
standard: ICH E9(R1) estimand addendum; ICH E9; ICH M11 (estimand placement in protocol); ICH E3
qc: Independent biostatistician review of estimand completeness (all 5 attributes, every ICE addressed); clinical & clin-pharm alignment review; consistency check objectives↔estimands↔endpoints↔analysis sets
ai_use: Cloud Claude drafts the objective→estimand→endpoint table and enumerates candidate intercurrent events and handling strategies from de-identified design text; biostatistician selects/decides ICE strategies and population-level summaries. Estimand definitions are human-decided; AI is a scaffolding/consistency aid only.

deliverable: Statistical considerations / analysis-overview section of protocol
description: The protocol's statistical-methods overview: high-level description of planned analyses by domain (PK NCA, popPK/exposure-response, safety/tolerability, PD), descriptive-statistics conventions, handling of BLQ/missing data at a summary level, decision/escalation logic reference, and an explicit statement that full detail resides in the SAP. Sets reader expectations without pre-empting the SAP. Includes statement on software (Phoenix WinNonlin for NCA; SAS/R for summaries; NONMEM/Monolix for popPK).
inputs: Design rationale; estimand framework; endpoint list; PK/PD analysis intent; safety review structure; sponsor SOPs/templates
outputs: Statistical-considerations section of protocol; precursor and contract for the full SAP; informs DSMB/SRC charter
standard: ICH E9 / E9(R1); ICH M11 statistical-considerations section; ICH E3 (consistency with future CSR methods); ICH E6(R3)
qc: Peer review by independent biostatistician; alignment check vs estimands and vs planned SAP scope; cross-functional protocol review
ai_use: Cloud Claude drafts and tightens the statistical-overview prose from de-identified protocol content and templates, and checks it does not contradict the estimand/design sections; biostatistician owns scope decisions. Pure document drafting — no reported numbers.

description: Defines the analysis sets to be used: e.g., All-Enrolled/Screened, Safety (all subjects who received any study drug), PK/PK-evaluable (received drug with at least one quantifiable concentration and no protocol deviations affecting PK such as emesis within X×Tmax), PD-evaluable, and (for crossover substudies) Completers/BE-evaluable per period. Specifies the rules that move a subject in/out of each set and links each estimand's population attribute to a concrete analysis set. Protocol gives definitions; SAP operationalizes derivations.
inputs: Estimand population attributes; design (sentinel/cohort/crossover structure); PK-exclusion criteria (emesis rules, dosing errors, missed samples); anticipated intercurrent events
outputs: Analysis-populations subsection of protocol; basis for ADSL population flags and SAP analysis-set derivation specs; drives every TLF's population footnote
standard: ICH E9 / E9(R1); ICH M11; CDISC ADaMIG v1.3 (population-flag conventions, e.g., SAFFL/PKFL); ICH E3
qc: Independent review of set definitions for mutual consistency and estimand linkage; clin-pharm review of PK-evaluable exclusion rules; traceability check to ADSL flag plan
ai_use: Cloud Claude drafts standardized analysis-set definitions consistent with ADaMIG flag conventions and proposes PK-exclusion edge cases to consider; biostatistician finalizes the rules. Definitional text only; no subject data.

deliverable: Sample-size / power justification (or documented rationale for no formal sizing in FIH)
description: For FIH SAD/MAD, typically a documented rationale that the cohort size (e.g., 8 subjects: 6 active + 2 placebo) is selected for safety observation and PK characterization rather than hypothesis-test power, with PK-precision considerations (expected within-subject CV%, resulting precision/half-width of CIs on geometric-mean PK parameters, probability of observing an AE of a given background rate). For comparative substudies (food-effect, DDI, relative-BA/BE), a formal power/precision calculation: power to conclude equivalence within 80–125% given assumed GMR and intra-subject CV%, or expected 90% CI half-width. Documents all assumptions and software/method.
inputs: Design (crossover vs parallel, cohort size, active:placebo ratio); assumed intra-/inter-subject CV% from class/prior data; assumed GMR and equivalence margins for comparative arms; target AE-detection probability; precision targets
outputs: Sample-size/justification subsection of protocol; assumptions carried into SAP; informs feasibility and operational planning
standard: ICH E9 (sample size & assumptions); ICH M11; FDA BA/BE & food-effect guidance; EMA bioequivalence/early-phase guidance; ICH E9(R1) (sizing tied to estimand)
qc: Independent re-derivation/double-check of sample-size or precision calculation (independent code/program run); review of assumptions vs source data; documentation of method and seed/software version
ai_use: Cloud Claude drafts the narrative and can scaffold the calculation approach on de-identified assumptions; the actual sample-size/precision/power numbers are produced and confirmed by a validated stats engine (SAS/R/nQuery/PASS) and independently re-derived by a second biostatistician — AI output is one side of a double-check, never the reported number. Amber because numbers influence the design.

deliverable: Randomization design & specification (incl. sentinel dosing & active:placebo allocation)
description: The randomization design document/spec: method (e.g., permuted blocks within cohort to maintain the active:placebo ratio such as 6:2), stratification (cohort/period), allocation concealment and blinding scheme (double-blind to active vs placebo; PK reviewers may be unblinded under firewall), sentinel-dosing scheme (1 active + 1 placebo dosed first, observation window before remaining cohort), and crossover sequence randomization (e.g., Williams design) for food-effect/DDI/BE. Defines seed management, list format, who generates, who holds the blind, and emergency-unblinding pathway. The schedule itself is generated later (Stage 2) and is RED.
inputs: Design (cohorts, sequences, sentinel scheme, ratio); blinding plan; IWRS/IRT capabilities; dose-escalation structure; number of cohorts/periods
outputs: Randomization specification appendix to protocol; instructions for randomization-schedule generation (Stage 2) and IWRS setup; basis for unblinding/code-break plan
standard: ICH E9 (randomization & blinding); ICH E6(R3) GCP; ICH M11; 21 CFR Part 11 (controlled, audit-trailed list generation); sponsor randomization SOP
qc: Independent biostatistician review of design spec; verification that block/ratio/sentinel logic enforces intended allocation; segregation-of-duties check (spec author ≠ schedule generator); blinding-integrity review
ai_use: Cloud Claude drafts the randomization-design specification text (block/sentinel/sequence logic, blinding & unblinding narrative) from de-identified design parameters; biostatistician owns the design. NOTE: generation of the actual randomization schedule (the unblinded list) is a separate Stage-2 deliverable that is RED — validated engine + human only, zero LLM in the path.

deliverable: DSMB/SRC/SEC charter — statistical decision rules & dose-escalation criteria
description: Biostatistics contribution to the Safety Review Committee / Safety Evaluation Committee / DSMB charter: the quantitative dose-escalation decision rules (what safety/PK/tolerability data are reviewed before escalating; stopping/halting rules; max-exposure caps relative to nonclinical margins; criteria to repeat, de-escalate, or stop a dose), data flow to the committee (blinded vs unblinded views, who is unblinded, firewall), cadence of review, and the statistical/PK summaries the committee receives at each gate. Specifies the predefined escalation algorithm (rule-based n+m, or model-based such as BLRM/mTPI/CRM if used).
inputs: Design & dose levels; nonclinical exposure margins/NOAEL; predicted human PK; safety stopping-rule philosophy; escalation method choice; committee composition & governance SOP
outputs: Statistical/escalation sections of the DSMB/SRC charter; drives interim/escalation data packages, randomization/unblinding firewall, and SAP escalation-summary specs
standard: ICH E9 (data monitoring, decision rules); ICH E6(R3) GCP; FDA guidance on Data Monitoring Committees; FIH guidance (escalation/stopping); ICH M11
qc: Independent review of decision-rule logic for unambiguity and safety conservatism; consistency check vs protocol dose levels and stopping rules; committee/regulatory review; (if model-based) operating-characteristics simulation review
ai_use: Cloud Claude drafts charter prose and decision-rule logic on de-identified text (Green/Amber). When the rules are illustrated against any subject-level or unblinded escalation data, that work routes to the LOCAL frozen model (zero egress). Escalation decisions and any reported safety/PK numbers come from validated engines + the committee/human — AI is out of the decision path. Amber because the rules govern dosing of humans.

description: Simulation study characterizing the chosen escalation design before the study starts: for rule-based designs, probability of escalating/stopping under assumed true safety/PK scenarios; for model-based designs (BLRM/mTPI/CRM/EWOC), operating characteristics — probability of selecting/declaring each dose as acceptable, probability of overdosing beyond target toxicity interval, expected sample size, and sensitivity to priors. Produces tables/figures that justify the design's safety and efficiency to the team and regulators. Includes reproducibility metadata (seeds, software, scenario grid).
inputs: Escalation rule/model & priors; dose levels & cohort sizes; assumed dose-toxicity / dose-exposure scenarios; target toxicity interval / escalation thresholds; nonclinical predictions
outputs: Operating-characteristics tables/figures supporting the protocol design section and DSMB/SRC charter; design justification for regulators
standard: ICH E9 / E9(R1); FDA adaptive-design & FIH guidance; reproducibility per 21 CFR Part 11 (versioned, seeded code); sponsor simulation SOP
qc: Independent re-run/replication of simulation with documented seed & environment; code review; scenario-grid completeness review; sanity-check of OC against design intuition
ai_use: Cloud Claude (on de-identified, simulated parameters — no PHI) helps scaffold/refactor simulation code (R/SAS, packages such as escalation/dfcrm/UnifiedDoseFinding/OncoBayes2) and draft the OC interpretation; all simulation results are produced by the validated stats engine and the code is independently re-run/reviewed. AI is a coding assistant and one side of a double-check, not the source of reported operating characteristics. Amber.

description: Where the design includes adaptive features beyond simple sequential escalation — e.g., blinded or unblinded sample-size re-estimation, adaptive dose selection, cohort-size adaptation, adding/dropping a cohort, optional food-effect/DDI cohort triggered by emerging PK, or interim popPK look — this deliverable specifies the adaptation rules, decision points, statistical methods, Type-I-error/operating-characteristic control, firewall/independence of the analysis, and information flow. If the study has no adaptive elements, this is a documented 'not applicable' statement. Frequently minimal in FIH but explicitly addressed.
inputs: Design objectives; escalation/interim structure; nonclinical & emerging-PK expectations; regulatory appetite for adaptation; DSMB/SRC charter
outputs: Adaptive-design subsection of protocol & SAP; informs interim-analysis specs, firewall/unblinding plan, and operating-characteristics simulation
standard: ICH E9 / E9(R1); FDA Adaptive Designs for Clinical Trials guidance; EMA reflection paper on adaptive designs; ICH M11; 21 CFR Part 11
qc: Independent statistical review of adaptation rules & error-rate control; simulation of operating characteristics; firewall/independence review; documentation of 'N/A' decision where no adaptation
ai_use: Cloud Claude drafts the adaptive-rule specification and N/A rationale on de-identified design text, and helps scaffold any supporting simulation code; error-control results come from a validated engine with independent replication. AI assists drafting/structuring; the adaptation rules and their statistical validity are human-owned. Amber because adaptations affect inference & dosing.

deliverable: PK precision / variability assumptions memo (CV%, BLQ/LLOQ & sampling-scheme adequacy)
description: Supporting analytical memo underpinning the sample-size/PK-rationale: documents assumed within- and between-subject CV% for key PK parameters (from class/nonclinical/literature), the LLOQ and expected BLQ pattern, and an assessment that the planned PK sampling schedule adequately characterizes Cmax/Tmax/AUC/terminal-phase t1/2 (e.g., enough samples in absorption and terminal phases, sampling beyond ~3–5 half-lives for adequate AUC extrapolation). Justifies that the design yields PK estimates of acceptable precision. Often co-authored with the PK scientist.
inputs: Predicted human PK (nonclinical/allometry/PBPK); assay LLOQ; literature/class CV%; proposed sampling times; AUC-extrapolation acceptance criteria
outputs: Assumptions feeding the sample-size rationale and SAP NCA/BLQ-handling rules; justification of sampling schedule in protocol; informs ADPC/ADPP planning
standard: ICH E9; FDA/EMA PK and BA/BE guidance; sponsor NCA/PK SOP; reproducible assumptions
qc: Joint biostatistician–PK scientist review; cross-check sampling adequacy vs predicted t1/2; sensitivity of precision to CV% assumptions; documentation of assumption sources
ai_use: Cloud Claude (de-identified literature/parameters) summarizes class CV% and drafts the memo, and sanity-checks sampling-scheme adequacy against predicted half-lives; PK scientist and biostatistician own the assumptions and any precision figures (validated-engine-confirmed if quantitative). No subject data. Green for the narrative; any quantitative precision result is double-checked.

description: Formal, documented biostatistical review and approval of the final protocol (and any protocol amendments): verifies internal consistency of objectives↔estimands↔endpoints↔analysis sets↔planned analyses; checks sample-size/precision rationale, randomization/blinding, dose-escalation rules, and statistical-considerations text are correct, feasible, and mutually consistent; confirms alignment with ICH E9(R1) and M11. Captured as a tracked review with comment resolution and a signature in the protocol approval/QC record. Includes review of amendments with version control.
inputs: Final draft protocol & amendments; estimand framework; sample-size & randomization specs; DSMB/SRC charter; sponsor review/sign-off SOP
outputs: Signed biostatistics review/approval record; comment-resolution log; gates protocol finalization and start of SAP/spec development
standard: ICH E9 / E9(R1); ICH M11; ICH E6(R3) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11 (electronic signature & audit trail); sponsor protocol-approval SOP
qc: Comment-tracking & resolution log; independent (second-biostatistician) consistency check on objectives↔estimands↔endpoints; signed/audited approval; amendment version control
ai_use: Cloud Claude runs an automated consistency/completeness check across the de-identified protocol (objective↔estimand↔endpoint↔analysis-set↔analysis traceability; ICH E9(R1)/M11 checklist) and drafts review comments; the biostatistician adjudicates every comment and provides the human sign-off (Part 11 e-signature). AI assists review, never approves. Green.

description: Protocol/charter-level definition of the emergency-unblinding pathway and blinding-firewall: who may unblind, under what medical circumstances, the IWRS/IRT mechanism, the firewall keeping the blinded study team blinded while authorized unblinded PK/safety reviewers (and DSMB/SRC) operate behind a controlled barrier, and how unblinding events are logged and reported. Establishes the governance the executable code-break (generated/operated in later stages) must follow. Defines the segregation of duties for blinded vs unblinded roles.
inputs: Blinding scheme & randomization design; DSMB/SRC firewall needs; IWRS capabilities; pharmacovigilance/SUSAR reporting requirements; sponsor unblinding SOP
outputs: Unblinding/firewall section of protocol & DSMB charter; requirements for the Stage-2/4 code-break execution and audit log
standard: ICH E6(R3) GCP; ICH E9 (blinding integrity); 21 CFR Part 11 (audit trail of unblinding); pharmacovigilance regs; sponsor unblinding SOP
qc: Independent review of firewall & segregation-of-duties; verification unblinding pathway is auditable and minimally disruptive to the blind; cross-check vs DSMB charter and randomization spec
ai_use: Cloud Claude drafts the de-identified unblinding/firewall procedure and governance text; any illustration touching subject-level allocation would route to the LOCAL model. The actual unblinding/code-break execution (later stages) is RED — validated/audited mechanism, zero LLM. Here AI only drafts the procedural specification. Green for the document.

deliverable: Blinded Sample-Size Re-Estimation (BSSR) Plan — design-stage provision
description: Pre-specified design-stage provision for blinded sample-size re-estimation (e.g., nuisance-parameter / variance re-estimation for PK variability or PD endpoint), distinct from the initial sample-size justification already listed. Even in FIH, MAD-cohort or PD-powered substudies may warrant a blinded re-estimation rule. Must define trigger, who performs it (blinded statistician), what is recomputed, and how Type I error / blinding is protected. Absent this, any mid-study cohort-size change looks ad hoc to an inspector.
standard: ICH E9 / E9(R1); ICH E6(R3) GCP
inputs: Protocol design, PK/PD variability assumptions memo, estimand definitions
outputs: BSSR plan section (in protocol or SAP), blinded re-estimation SOP reference
ai_use: GREEN — AI drafts plan scaffold on de-identified design text
ai_engine: Cloud Claude (API+BAA) on de-identified design text
qc: Biostatistician review and sign-off; no unblinded data touched

deliverable: Estimand-to-design traceability & assumptions log (intercurrent-event strategy register)
description: A standalone register documenting, per estimand, the five attributes (treatment, population, variable, intercurrent-event handling strategy, population-level summary) and the rationale/assumptions for each — particularly intercurrent-event strategies (e.g., treatment discontinuation, rescue, dropout) for PK/PD and safety estimands. The current list aligns objectives->estimands->endpoints but does not capture the intercurrent-event strategy register and assumptions audit trail that E9(R1) inspections probe.
standard: ICH E9(R1) estimand framework
inputs: Protocol objectives, endpoint definitions, analysis-population definitions
outputs: Estimand attribute table with intercurrent-event strategies and assumptions
ai_use: GREEN — drafting/structuring on de-identified text
qc: Biostatistician + medical reviewer sign-off

description: The definitive, signed statistical analysis plan operationalizing the protocol's statistical section: objectives->estimands->endpoints (ICH E9(R1)), analysis sets, all analysis methods (descriptive PK/PD/safety, any inferential models for food-effect/DDI/BE/QTc), handling of missing data and intercurrent events, multiplicity, BLQ/LLOQ rules cross-referenced to the PK plan, planned TLF inventory mapping. Finalized and approved BEFORE database lock/unblinding.
inputs: Final protocol + amendments, ICH E9(R1) estimand framework, DSMB/SRC charter, regulatory feedback, ADaM spec scope, TLF shells
outputs: Authoritative source for programming specs, TLF shells, ADaM specs, analysis-population derivation, and Stage 4 analysis execution; CSR 16.1.9 appendix
standard: ICH E9 + E9(R1) estimands, ICH E3 (alignment with CSR), ICH E6(R3) GCP, ICH M11; version-controlled, dated, signed
qc: Senior biostatistician peer review; cross-check vs protocol & estimands; documented sign-off; formal version control of amendments
ai_use: Cloud Claude drafts/structures SAP prose, checks internal consistency vs protocol, generates estimand tables and method boilerplate on de-identified text; ALL statistical method decisions, final wording, and sign-off are human (lead biostatistician). No PHI to cloud.

description: Formal change-control record for every SAP revision: rationale, what changed (estimands, methods, populations, TLF scope), timing relative to unblinding (pre- vs post-unblinding flagged), approval signatures. Ensures any post-hoc/post-unblinding changes are transparently documented for inspection.
inputs: Prior SAP versions, protocol amendments, regulatory/DSMB input, change requests
outputs: Audit trail for CSR 16.1.9 and inspection; integrity evidence that primary analyses were pre-specified
standard: ICH E9, ICH E3, ICH E6(R3), 21 CFR Part 11 (audit trail), ALCOA++
qc: Independent review of each change; timestamp vs lock/unblinding verified; Part 11 audit trail retained
ai_use: Cloud Claude drafts change summaries and redline narratives on de-identified text; human owns approval and the pre-/post-unblinding classification.

description: Precise, programmable definitions of all analysis sets and their derivation logic: Enrolled/Randomized, Safety, PK (PK-evaluable / PK Concentration / PK Parameter), PD, Per-Protocol/Completers, DDI/Food-Effect/BE sets as applicable; rules for excluding subjects/profiles from PK and the decision authority/timing (typically at blinded data review).
inputs: Protocol populations, SAP, estimand intercurrent-event handling, PK exclusion rules
outputs: ADSL population flags (SAFFL, PKFL/APKFL, PPROTFL, etc.); drives every TLF denominator
standard: ADaMIG v1.3 (population flags), ICH E9(R1), CDISC CT
qc: Independent reviewer confirms flag logic vs SAP; double-programmed in ADSL; reconciled at blinded data review
ai_use: Cloud Claude drafts decision-tree logic and flag-derivation pseudocode on de-identified definitions; final exclusion rules and population calls are human/documented at blinded review.

deliverable: TLF Shells / Mock-Up Shells (Tables, Listings, Figures)
description: Complete shell library: every planned table, listing, and figure with final titles, column headers, row structure, population footnotes, formatting/decimal conventions, programmer notes, and dummy/placeholder values. Covers disposition, demographics/baseline, exposure/compliance, deviations, PK concentration tables, mean conc-time figures (linear/semilog), NCA parameter tables, dose-proportionality, food-effect/DDI/BE, C-QTc, safety (TEAEs, SAEs, labs, vitals, ECG), PD, immunogenicity, listings.
inputs: SAP, protocol, TLF inventory, ADaM spec, sponsor/CRO output conventions
outputs: Programming target for Stage 4 TLF production; reviewed by clinical/medical writing for CSR alignment
standard: ICH E3 (in-text/appendix structure), sponsor output standards, CDISC CT for terminology
qc: Peer/medical-writer/clinical review of shells; titles/footnotes vs SAP; dummy-data render test for layout
ai_use: Cloud Claude scaffolds shell titles/footnotes/structure and proposes the full TLF inventory from the SAP (de-identified); human finalizes content and conventions. No real data.

description: Master numbered index of all tables, listings, and figures (with output numbers, titles, population, and SAP cross-reference), defining what is in-text vs appendix and the section mapping to the CSR. Drives scope, tracking, and QC completeness checks.
inputs: SAP, TLF shells, ICH E3 structure
outputs: Production tracking checklist; CSR section mapping; QC completeness ledger
standard: ICH E3, sponsor standards
qc: Cross-check vs SAP analyses (no orphan/missing outputs); peer review
ai_use: Cloud Claude generates and reconciles the inventory against the SAP; human approves scope.

description: Subject-level analysis dataset spec: one record per subject; all population flags, treatment variables (TRTxxP/A), reference dates (RFSTDTC/TRTSDT), demographics, baseline characteristics, disposition, stratification, and key derivations with metadata. Foundation for all BDS datasets.
inputs: SAP, analysis-set definitions, SDTM DM/EX/DS/SV, CT
outputs: ADSL dataset (Stage 3); merge source for all BDS ADaM; population denominators
standard: ADaMIG v1.3, CDISC CT, Define-XML v2.1, traceability to SDTM
qc: Spec peer review; downstream independent double-programming of ADSL; Pinnacle 21 ADaM conformance
ai_use: Cloud Claude drafts variable-level metadata and derivation text from SAP/SDTM annotations (de-identified spec text); human reviews. The dataset BUILD from real data (Stage 3) is Amber/double-programmed.

description: BDS spec for PK concentration analysis data: nominal vs actual sampling times, relative time variables (e.g., ARELTM/NRRELTM), analyte, concentration with units, BLQ/LLOQ flags and imputation rules, profile/exclusion flags, baseline handling. The analysis-ready bridge from SDTM PC to NCA input.
inputs: SAP/PK analysis plan (BLQ rules, nominal vs actual time), SDTM PC, bioanalytical LLOQ, ADSL
outputs: ADPC dataset; input to Phoenix WinNonlin NCA; concentration tables/figures
standard: ADaMIG v1.3, CDISC CT, Define-XML v2.1, ICH PK reporting practice
qc: Spec review vs PK plan (BLQ/time rules); independent double-programming of ADPC; reconciliation to SDTM PC
ai_use: Cloud Claude drafts BLQ/time-window derivation logic and metadata on de-identified spec; human owns the BLQ/LLOQ rules. Build & reported values are validated-engine/double-programmed downstream.

description: BDS spec for derived NCA PK parameters (Cmax, Tmax, AUC0-t/0-inf, t1/2, CL/F, Vz/F, %AUCextrap, lambda-z, accumulation) carried into analysis: parameter naming/CT, units, dose/period/treatment structure, and rules linking Phoenix outputs to the analysis dataset (Phoenix is the engine of record for the numbers).
inputs: SAP/PK plan (parameter list), ADPC, Phoenix WinNonlin NCA output structure, ADSL
outputs: ADPP dataset; NCA summary tables, dose-proportionality, food-effect/DDI/BE analyses, exposure-response covariates
standard: ADaMIG v1.3, CDISC CT (PK parameters), Define-XML v2.1
qc: Spec review vs parameter list; ADPP reconciled to validated Phoenix output; independent double-programming of structure
ai_use: Cloud Claude drafts the parameter mapping spec/metadata (de-identified). Reported PK parameter VALUES come ONLY from validated Phoenix WinNonlin — never an LLM; LLM stays out of the numeric path.

description: Occurrence-data-structure (OCCDS) spec for adverse events: MedDRA SOC/PT coding linkage, treatment-emergent flag derivation (relative to dosing), severity/relationship/seriousness, on-treatment windows, and one-record-per-event structure for TEAE summaries.
inputs: SAP, SDTM AE, MedDRA dictionary version, ADSL (TRTSDT), exposure
outputs: ADAE dataset; TEAE tables, SAE/deaths, AE listings
standard: ADaMIG v1.3 (OCCDS), MedDRA, CDISC CT, Define-XML v2.1
qc: Spec review of TEAE logic; independent double-programming; reconcile vs SDTM AE
ai_use: Cloud Claude drafts TEAE-flag and windowing logic on de-identified spec; human reviews coding-version/derivation. Build double-programmed.

deliverable: ADaM Dataset Specifications (ADLB / ADVS / ADEG — Labs, Vitals, ECG)
description: BDS specs for safety lab chemistry/hematology/urinalysis (ADLB), vital signs (ADVS), and ECG (ADEG): baseline derivation, change-from-baseline, analysis visit/time windowing, unit standardization (SI), reference ranges, normal-range/CTCAE flags, and (ADEG) QT/QTc method variables (Fridericia/Bazett) and shift categories.
inputs: SAP, SDTM LB/VS/EG, CT, reference ranges/CTCAE, QTc method per protocol/SAP
outputs: ADLB/ADVS/ADEG datasets; safety summary tables, shift tables, C-QTc input
standard: ADaMIG v1.3, CDISC CT, Define-XML v2.1
qc: Spec review of baseline/window/unit logic; independent double-programming; SDTM reconciliation
ai_use: Cloud Claude drafts baseline/windowing/unit-conversion and QTc-derivation logic on de-identified spec; human owns clinical conventions. Build double-programmed.

description: BDS spec for PD/biomarker endpoints: parameter definitions, baseline and change/percent-change derivations, analysis time windows, and structure to support PD summaries and exposure-response (PK/PD) covariate linkage.
inputs: SAP/PD plan, SDTM PD/biomarker domains, ADSL, ADPC (for PK/PD linkage)
outputs: ADPD dataset; PD summary tables/figures, exposure-response inputs
standard: ADaMIG v1.3, CDISC CT, Define-XML v2.1
qc: Spec review vs PD plan; independent double-programming; SDTM reconciliation
ai_use: Cloud Claude drafts PD derivation/metadata on de-identified spec; human reviews. Build double-programmed.

description: Source-to-SDTM mapping document defining how each CRF/raw field maps to SDTM domain variables (DM, PC, PP, EX, AE, LB, VS, EG, DS, MH, CM, PD/biomarker, DV), including controlled-terminology assignments, derivations, --SEQ/RELREC linkage, and SUPPQUAL handling. Drives SDTM build and the aCRF.
inputs: Final CRF/eCRF, EDC data structure, SDTMIG, CT, protocol, data-transfer specs
outputs: SDTM datasets (Stage 3), aCRF, SDTM Define-XML metadata source
standard: CDISC SDTMIG v3.4, Controlled Terminology, Define-XML v2.1
qc: Independent review vs SDTMIG/CT; Pinnacle 21 rule mapping; traceability check raw->SDTM
ai_use: Cloud Claude proposes mappings/CT assignments from de-identified CRF/spec text and flags SDTMIG gaps; human SDTM expert finalizes. Build & conformance are validated/double-programmed downstream.

description: The blank CRF annotated with SDTM variable/domain mappings and CT, used as the human-readable traceability link between collected data and SDTM. Prepared/reviewed against the final CRF and mapping spec; required submission artifact.
inputs: Final blank CRF, SDTM mapping spec, SDTMIG/CT
outputs: Submission aCRF; origin metadata for Define-XML; reviewer-guide input
standard: CDISC SDTMIG v3.4, Define-XML v2.1, CDISC aCRF conventions
qc: Page-by-page review vs mapping spec; consistency with Define-XML origin; peer review

description: Detailed programmer-facing derivation specifications underpinning ADaM and TLFs: algorithmic derivation logic, variable-level metadata, value-level metadata, formats/lengths, and explicit rules for complex derivations (windowing, baseline, TEAE, BLQ, dose-normalization). description: The detailed non-compartmental analysis specification (standalone or SAP appendix): full NCA parameter list (Cmax, Tmax, AUC0-t/0-inf/tau, t1/2, lambda-z, CL/F, Vz/F, accumulation, %extrap); BLQ/LLOQ handling rules (pre-/post-first-dose, embedded vs terminal); nominal vs actual time rules; lambda-z selection criteria and acceptance (adj R2, %extrap thresholds); profile/parameter exclusion rules; Phoenix WinNonlin settings/templates as engine of record.

description: Generation of the validated, reproducible randomization schedule (and any sentinel/cohort/dose-escalation allocation): block/stratification structure per design, seed and software documented, schedule kept restricted/secured, and loaded to IRT/IWRS. This is the engine of record for reported PK parameters.

deliverable: PK parameter (NCA) summary tables (Cmax, Tmax, AUC, t1/2, CL/F, Vz/F)

description: Dose-proportionality assessment (power model / slope with CI, or ANOVA on dose-normalized exposures) across SAD dose levels, and accumulation ratio (Racc) for Cmax/AUC between Day 1 and steady state in MAD, with relevant statistics and figures.
